##NAMES=Ruitenbergite
##RRUFFID=R110152
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Ca_9_B_26_O_34_(OH)_24_Cl_4_·13H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Potash Corporation of Saskatchewan mine, Penobsquis, Kings County, New Brunswick, Canada
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Donald M Doell and Brian Roulston
##DESCRIPTION=Pale yellow granular, extracted by dissolving the matrix of sylvite/halite, associated with strontioginorite R110149, howlite R110150, volkovskite R110151, congolite R110153, and hilgardite
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R110152
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 19.927 b: 9.7129 c: 17.559 alpha: 90 beta: 114.728 gamma: 90 volume: 3087 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
